data.title: Mark Zuckerberg's Meta Faces Backlash As Laid-Off Workers Say They Were Unfairly Targeted Despite Strong Performance Records
data.description: Meta is facing backlash after laying off 3,600 employees, with former workers alleging they were unfairly targeted despite strong performance records, including those on parental and medical leave, raising concerns about corporate ethics and cost-cutting motives.

data.title: Elon Musk's Boring Company To Build 'Dubai Loop' To Handle Upto 100,000 Passengers Per Hour, Slashing Travel Time With Electric Vehicles
data.description: The Roads and Transport Authority of Dubai has entered into a Memorandum of Understanding with ‘The Boring Company‘ to put up a loop system in Dubai, the Elon Musk-owned company said in a statement on Thursday.

data.title: 'Intel On A Tear,' Says Analyst As Stock Jumps Over 7% On Thursday Amid 18A Process Node Optimism
data.description: This Intel analyst has dismissed speculation surrounding the stock, pointing to strong fundamentals and terming recent jump as "an easy one."

data.title: Trump Administration Reevaluates CHIPS Act Grants, Potentially Delaying Semiconductor Funding: Report
data.description: The U.S. CHIPS and Science Act 2022 awards are reportedly up for renegotiation by the White House, which could result in a delay in some semiconductor disbursements.
